title: Vibrant Tandoori Australian Olives Flatbread
description: Mustardy, flaky flatbread infused with pitted black olives and tandoori spices, baked on a cedar-plank for a low-calorie Australian twist.
instructions: In a bowl combine whole wheat flour, active dry yeast, and salt.
Add warm water and olive oil to the dry mix and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 6 minutes until smooth.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise for 20 minutes until doubled.
Preheat the oven to 220°C and place a cedar-plank on the middle rack.
Roll the risen dough into a flat circle about 3 mm thick and transfer to a parchment-lined tray.
Brush the dough with low-fat yogurt, sprinkle tandoori spice mix, crushed mustard seeds, lemon zest, and fresh coriander.
Arrange pitted black olives evenly across the surface, then drizzle a thin layer of lemon juice.
Bake the flatbread on the cedar-plank for 15 minutes until edges are golden and the surface feels flaky.
Remove from oven, let cool for 5 minutes, then slice and serve warm.
